In this image Babylon represents a real historical organization. Nephi saw the devil establish it among the nations and kingdoms of the Gentiles (see 1 Ne. 13:4-6), and he traced its continual existence into the present (see 1 Ne. 13:26-29; 14:1-17). The bands of her neck are the curses of God upon her. When the inhabi tants of Israel were taken captive and led away into other lands, bands of enslavement were placed upon them. In addition to binding them physically, these bands also symbolized the spiritual bondage and captivity that lay ahead of them. Nephi saw in vision also the coming of the Pilgrims, who came to escape religious persecution. He foresaw the coming to America of peoples from many nations, their wars and contentions. As Nephi said, they did humble themselves before the Lord and "the power of God was with them, and also that the wrath of God was upon all those that were gathered together against them to battle. The Book of Mormon establishes better than any other book the plain and precious truths of the gospel, many of which have been lost from the Bible.
